mechanism: Inhibits multiple tyrosine kinases including FLT3 and BCR‑ABL
additional_notes: Clinically used multi‑kinase inhibitor; has FLT3 activity and can be potent in some AML lines.

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, disrupting protein degradation and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Proteasome inhibitor with reported activity in AML blasts and cell lines.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increasing oxidative stress and cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Repurposed anti‑rheumatic with reported oxidative‑stress–mediated cytotoxicity in AML.

mechanism: Multiple actions reported (mitochondrial uncoupling, STAT3 inhibition, Wnt/β‑catenin effects) leading to antiproliferative effects
additional_notes: Reported anti‑AML activity via multiple pathways; solubility and bioavailability can affect assays.

mechanism: Forms cytotoxic complexes (often copper‑dependent) that promote oxidative stress and cell death
additional_notes: Activity in vitro frequently requires copper(II) co‑treatment; test ±Cu as separate arms.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway, reducing MAPK signaling–driven proliferation
additional_notes: MAPK pathway inhibitor; potency can be low‑nM in sensitive contexts but cell‑line dependent.

mechanism: Allosteric inhibition of mTORC1, suppressing mTOR signaling and cell growth
additional_notes: Rapalog with reported effects on AML metabolism/growth; expected potency ~10–500 nM in some assays.

mechanism: Potent inhibition of multiple HDACs leading to histone acetylation and growth arrest/apoptosis
additional_notes: More potent pan‑HDAC inhibitor than SAHA in many cell assays; reported AML activity.

mechanism: Inhibits transcriptional CDKs (including CDK9), downregulating short‑lived survival proteins such as MCL‑1
additional_notes: Also known as Flavopiridol; has clinical history in AML and expected potency ~10–500 nM in some assays.

mechanism: Covalent inhibition of BTK and some off‑target kinases, modulating B‑cell receptor and related signaling
additional_notes: BTK inhibitor with variable activity in AML; can show cell‑line dependent sensitivity and synergy with other agents.

mechanism: Disrupts microtubule polymerization and has other anticancer mechanisms leading to mitotic arrest and apoptosis
additional_notes: Repurposed anticancer candidate with reported activity against leukemia cells; solubility considerations may apply.

mechanism: Alters cellular metabolism (AMPK activation, complex I inhibition) leading to metabolic stress; cytotoxicity in vitro often requires high concentrations
additional_notes: In vitro activity typically requires mM concentrations that exceed therapeutic plasma levels; interpret with caution.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase in the mevalonate pathway, which can induce apoptosis or sensitize AML cells
additional_notes: Statin with reported pro‑apoptotic/sensitizing effects in AML; cell‑line dependent potency (~1–10 µM reported).
